108.status: Injured his hand while sliding into home plate in sixth inning of March 14 game against Seattle. Said on March 15 that he “dodged a bullet” and should be out only a couple of days.
Will fly to Texas next week to be evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister. “There is a concern for the state of my UCL right now,” said Stephenson, who now hopes he’ll be “able to pitch this year.”
Exited in the first inning after striking out in his first at-bat March 11. Was replaced at shortstop in the bottom of the first for precautionary reasons.
Underwent labrum surgery on May 14. Has been throwing bullpens without any issues with his latest one coming on March 10, but appears likely to start the season on the injured list.

Diagnosed on Jan. 31 but was able to throw and eat normally while dealing with fatigue. Behind the other pitchers, but has been throwing bullpens with no issues and will progress to facing hitters next.

Underwent left hip surgery after attempting to rehab in the offseason and missed the 2025 season. Not going to play in '26 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Will be placed on the 60-day IL once the club needs the roster spot.

110.status: Threw first bullpen session on March 10. However, still has a low probability of being ready for Opening Day.
Underwent procedure to remove bone from right hand on Feb. 12. Stitches removed Feb. 23. Hitting ball off tee with two-handed swings as of March 7.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.
Felt soreness in right elbow while rehabbing right oblique injury. After testing, received platelet-rich plasma injection on Feb. 20.
Placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 14. No clear timeline for potential return yet.

111.status: Was scratched just before the March 15 game. Manager Alex Cora said Mayer's knee felt sore before the game and the team kept him out of the lineup. He should be back on March 17.

111.expectedReturn: Will miss significant time at start of season
111.status: Underwent left shoulder arthroscopic debridement surgery on March 12 and was placed on 60-day injured list. Had a PRP injection in early February, and has not participated in any baseball activity.
Casas said he won't progress enough to play Grapefruit League games. Waiting for clearance to run at full speed and slide.
Will throw two innings of live batting practice on March 12. Could get in a game soon thereafter.
Having a deload week, and will throw extended bullpen session March 12 rather than throw to live hitters. Will resume facing hitters the week of March 16.
Likely to require season-ending surgery, but is going to consult with Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles for another opinion.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Started gradual throwing program on flat ground in the second week of Spring Training.

112.status: Exited Japan’s WBC quarterfinal loss to Venezuela in the first inning due to “right knee discomfort” following a stolen-base attempt. Will be evaluated after rejoining Cubs in camp.
Sustained left elbow sprain in collision on defensive play at first base on Feb. 21. Returned to Cactus League game action as a designated hitter on March 14.

Medically cleared Feb. 28 by Dr. Keith Meister, who performed Steele's elbow surgery (left ulnar collateral ligament revision repair) on April 18, 2025. Threw 19 pitches in a live BP session on March 13.
Resumed playing catch on March 3 after pausing his throwing program for 10 days. Could return to a mound as soon as March 16, but expected to open the season on the IL.
Manager Craig Counsell noted on March 8 that Hodge will be shut down from throwing for a couple weeks before being re-evaluated. The righty is expected to open the season on the IL.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Cubs manager Craig Counsell said Austin could miss "months" while recovering.

113.status: An MRI revealed Ferguson's injury and he will not pitch for at least two weeks. He will undergo a second MRI in Cincinnati after camp breaks.
Greene was scheduled to have arthroscopic surgery March 11 to remove bone chips and loose bodies from his right elbow. He is expected to miss 14-16 weeks.

114.status: Jammed left shoulder on slide into third base on March 15 and has some inflammation. Considered day to day.
Threw bullpen sessions on March 12 and 15.
Has "mild" calf strain, per manager Stephen Vogt. Able to do some baseball activity but will not play for a few more days. Last played March 9.
Throwing bullpen sessions and from 120-150 feet on flat ground. Expected to progress to throwing live batting practice in second half of March and miss the start of '26 season.
Suffered moderate grade strain during Feb. 24 appearance against the Dodgers that is expected to sideline him for 6-8 weeks.

115.status: Injured his knee while sliding trying to make a catch in the outfield.
Rule 5 pick from the Tigers hasn't pitched since leaving a March 4 exhibition game against Team USA. Both he and the club are consulting with doctors about the next step.
Underwent an anti-inflammatory injection on March 1 and has thrown twice since, without pain. Doesn't have a date to return to mound sessions.
Scheduled to undergo ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
Targeting the middle of April to return to game action. Hopes to face hitters before the end of Spring Training.
Continues to undergo various treatments and therapies. Said he has been unable to progress to baseball activities.

116.status: Began a throwing program this week. Placed on 60-day IL on March 10.
Began a throwing progression on March 12. Completing a hitting progression.
Threw a live batting practice session on March 13.
Threw his first bullpen session on March 13.
Played catch out to 105 feet three times this week. Underwent Tommy John surgery on June 16.
Reported abdominal tightness after last outing on March 7. Will rehab daily.
Removed from Chinese Taipei roster for World Baseball Classic. Returned to Lakeland, Fla., on March 7 and underwent further tests on March 8. Optioned to Triple-A Toledo on March 9.

116.playerName: Bryce Rainer (MLB No. 35 overall prospect; Tigers' No. 3)
116.status: Underwent season-ending surgery after dislocating his shoulder diving back to first base in a June 3 game for Single-A Lakeland. Continuing rehab process this Spring Training. Should be ready early in season.

Underwent labrum repair surgery on right shoulder on Feb. 2, performed by Dr. Keith Meister, after offseason rehab program was unsuccessful. Expected to miss 2026 season but should be ready for Spring Training in 2027.

117.status: Hader, who was shut down in early February, will throw his third bullpen session March 17. He will begin the season on the injured list.
Scheduled to get into his first Grapefruit League game on March 17.
Was brought along slowly from offseason elbow surgery, but experienced elbow soreness and had his throwing program slowed down. Threw another bullpen session March 15.
Diagnosed March 5 with a fractured tip of his right ring finger, suffered during an exhibition game in the Dominican Republic on March 4. He will be reevaluated in two weeks.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Underwent surgery to repair his ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace as well as a repair of the flexor tendon in his forearm on June 6.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow May 23.

118.status: Exited after two innings March 13 against D-backs after feeling tightness in his right groin. Felt fine March 14. Manager Matt Quatraro expects India back in the lineup March 17.
Felt tightness following March 10 game. Will be held out of lineup next few days as precaution. Collins will play defense in a Minor League game March 15, then be reevaluated.
Suffered low-grade calf strain during a defensive play March 6. Massey is scheduled to hit in Minor League games March 15 and March 17.
Suffered Grade 1+ oblique strain on Feb. 25. Began playing catch again March 5 and is up to 75 feet with a little higher intensity.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Slow-played his offseason throwing program due to lingering discomfort. He threw a light bullpen session for the first time this spring on March 12.
Imaging revealed inflammation, but not structural damage, in Stone's shoulder. He's shut down from throwing for at least a couple weeks, after which he'll be re-evaluated.
Underwent shoulder debridement procedure in September 2025. Has progressed to throwing full bullpen sessions.
Velocity is down from where it is expected to be, so the Dodgers are slow-playing his ramp-up. He is only playing catch.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025. Began throwing off the mound to a standing catcher in late February, with the goal of progressing to full bullpen sessions during March.

120.status: Began taking swings on March 10. Faced Foster Griffin in an intrasquad game on March 15 and felt good.
Threw off the mound for the first time on March 13.
Began throwing progression and completed 25 throws at 60 feet.

120.playerName: Jarlin Susana (Nationals' No. 4 prospect, MLB No. 80)
120.status: Threw 15 pitches at a distance of 60 feet on Feb. 20; continuing arm care and throwing progression.

120.playerName: Travis Sykora (Nationals' No. 2 prospect, MLB No. 54)
120.status: Threw 15 pitches at a distance of 60 feet on Feb. 20; continuing arm care and throwing progression.

121.injury: Stress reaction in left hamate bone
121.status: Made his Grapefruit League debut on March 15, four and a half weeks after Feb. 11 surgery. Remains on track to be ready for Opening Day.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Exact timetable unknown, but will miss at least two months with what manager Carlos Mendoza called a "significant" injury.
Is about a month behind other Mets pitchers. Hopes to appear in a Grapefruit League game before the end of March and return by late April or early May.

121.injury: Thoracic outlet syndrome in right shoulder
121.status: Surgery has been recommended for Stock after he was diagnosed with arterial thoracic outlet syndrome, per MLB Network insider Jon Heyman. Stock told Heyman that he hopes to pitch before the end of the season.
Was scratched from his scheduled Grapefruit League start on March 9 after reporting some shoulder fatigue. Will continue to throw in non-game action.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: Continues to take at-bats on the Minor League side of camp as he builds towards a running progression. Could make his Cactus League debut next week.

134.status: Struck in the side of the head on Friday night by a 103 mph comebacker in the seventh inning. Left the field under his own power, being treated and evaluated by Pirates medical staff.
Participating in all baseball activities. Ready to play in Grapefruit League games and begin ramping up toward regular season.

Will continue to throw live BP as part of his rehab progression. Senior director of sports medicine Todd Tomczyk said Jones is meeting all the benchmarks. Goal is for Jones to be game-ready at the one-year mark post-surgery (May 21).

Will not throw for a couple of weeks after experiencing swelling and soreness following a live workout on Feb. 22. Will miss playing for Team Venezuela in the World Baseball Classic, but the Pirates are hopeful he'll be ready by the start of the regular season.

135.status: Wagner sustained the injury in early March, and as a result has only appeared in five Cactus League games.
Hoeing elected to undergo surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow and will miss the 2026 season.
Manager Craig Stammen said on March 12 that Song was doing defensive drills and baserunning and that he was “probably getting close” to swinging the bat again.

After missing the 2025 season, Musgrove returned to the mound for an exhibition against Team Great Britain. But he's being slow-played this spring, with manager Craig Stammen saying Musgrove was “in a little bit of a holding pattern” on March 12.

Matsui threw his first bullpen session on March 6 after he sustained the injury during a live batting practice session in February. He's yet to face hitters, and his status for Opening Day is in question.

Waldron likely won't be built up enough to make the Opening Day roster. But he resumed throwing bullpens in early March, and manager Craig Stammen noted that he expects Waldron to return for an appearance or two in the Cactus League.
Adam, who underwent quadriceps surgery in September, has targeted Opening Day for a return. He faced hitters for the first time on Feb. 20 and began doing fielding drills in early March.
Brito underwent UCL internal brace surgery as well as a flexor tendon repair operation in April 2025. He was placed on the 60-day IL in February but has resumed throwing bullpens in Padres camp.

Canning, whose 2025 season ended in June because of the injury, threw a high-intensity bullpen session on a back field on March 3. His next step could be facing hitters, though he hasn't yet been cleared for fielding drills.

Darvish underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. He will miss the 2026 season and has considered voiding the final three years of his contract.

136.status: Was out of the lineup for the fourth game in a row on March 15 vs. Reds, as issue resurfaced. Club will wait until after off-day on March 16 to reassess.

First full-effort bullpen cut short after discomfort during higher-intensity warmup pitches on March 11. Still cleared to play catch; will attempt another touch-and-feel bullpen in range of March 14-15.More »

136.expectedReturn: To the outfield, the week of March 16
136.status: Returned to lineup for first time in seven days on March 8 at designated hitter. Could progress to outfield again if all goes well. Had been experiencing fatigue in the same shoulder that he fractured last April.More »
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Weighing treatment options and will visit Dr. Keith Meister in Dallas to get a second opinion.
Departed March 14 game after hurting the hamstring while attempting to advance from first to third base. Will undergo an MRI on March 16.
Continuing his rehab progression and is currently throwing out to 90 feet.
Suffered a high grade strain of his right hip flexor while pitching for Team Colombia on March 3 and is expected to miss up to three months.
Began a throwing program on March 8 and has been cleared to DH in Cactus League games.

Missed entire 2025 season due to left shoulder surgery and then underwent right knee arthroscopy to address his meniscus in August. Has been throwing bullpen sessions but won't be ready for Opening Day.
Underwent surgery to repair the anterior capsule in his right shoulder in May 2025. Started a bullpen progression on Feb. 10 and is throwing twice per week.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025. Will begin a throwing progression in mid-March.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Dobbins will start the season on the injured list. He is able to pitch but is being held out of agility drills. The team is still mapping out the next steps of Dobbins' rehab.
Was scheduled to start March 7, but was a late scratch. Team is giving it time for recovery to run its course, per manager Oliver Marmol.
Nootbaar underwent surgery on Oct. 7. He arrived at Cardinals camp on Feb. 18 and said there is no timetable for his return to game action. However, he has not yet been ruled out for Opening Day.
Thompson underwent surgery in August to clean up his left shoulder. He is on a throwing program, but he won't be cleared to pitch in games until some point in the regular season.

139.status: “Didn’t feel great” when playing catch Feb. 19. Received a cortisone injection on Feb. 20. Threw a bullpen on March 14. Live BP planned around March 24-26, with a rehab assignment around the end of the month.
Wilson has not pitched in Rays camp, as he is dealing with a back issue that required an injection. He is not expected to be ready for Opening Day.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July. Placed on 60-day IL on Feb. 10.

140.status: Went 1-for-3 with a double and a walk on the backfields in Surprise, Ariz., on March 15. Skip Schumaker said he could be in the main game against the White Sox the following day.
Has not played since March 5 due to back spasms, but resumed taking swings on March 13. Skip Schumaker is hopeful he can return to the lineup against the White Sox on March 16.
Took a comebacker off the bat of Kyle Higashioka in batting practice and will be out for about four weeks.
Has thrown multiple bullpens since the start of camp. Should be ready for a Minor League rehab assignment as the 2026 season opens.
Continuing to rehab a shoulder injury that kept him sidelined for much of 2025. Should begin throwing bullpens before the end of camp.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.
Continuing to rehab a back injury that kept him sidelined for much of 2025. Behind in his throwing progression, but should return by the start of the Triple-A season.

141.status: Popped up when Berríos underwent MRI exam for the WBC. Follow-up MRI showed "different inflammation" from a year ago, but no ligaments are involved. Will visit Dr. Meister on Tuesday.

Was scheduled to begin throwing again March 10, which is a step in the right direction after being shut down the past couple of weeks. The Blue Jays were working to find some preventative measures to help Tiedemann avoid further elbow soreness in the future.

141.expectedReturn: Week to week with a "delayed" ramp-up
141.status: Working in a couple of "heavy" throw days each week, but has yet to return to the mound and it will remain difficult to chart a timeline until that happens.
Garcia is on a similar timeline to Bieber, working through some "heavy" throw days each week, but he has not thrown off a mound yet.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season. He is with the club now to begin his rehab in Florida.

142.playerName: Walker Jenkins (MIN No. 1 prospect, No. 14 overall)
142.status: Expected to return to Minor League games on or about March 20, and is likely to be available for Opening Day.
Progressing through workouts and has begun throwing a medicine ball, focusing on shoulder strength, with no issues. Hopes to resume throwing the week of March 16.
Has resumed normal workouts and hopes to resume throwing by the end of the March 13-15 weekend.
Threw a bullpen session on March 11 and was scheduled to throw another on March 13.

142.injury: “Significant tearing” in UCL in right elbow
142.status: Underwent surgery on Feb. 25 to repair, rather than reconstruct, the UCL and will miss the season.More >

143.status: Threw his first live batting practice session on March 14 and said it went well. Wheeler said he will throw another live BP on March 17, likely a simulated two-inning game.

He will pitch in his first spring game March 17, but it could be in a Minors game instead of a Grapefruit League game. Kerkering plans to pitch in three games before the end of camp. Based on his performance, he could be ready for Opening Day.

143.playerName: Aidan Miller (Phillies' No. 1 prospect, No. 23 overall)
143.status: Sent to Philadelphia on March 6 to see another team doctor about his recurring back issue. Manager Rob Thomson said there is zero concern and the team is being "super cautious." Miller was reassigned to Minor League camp March 12.
Had not played this spring before being optioned to Minor League camp on March 7. Had a PRP injection in the offseason, but he remains sidelined.
Left Team Israel in the World Baseball Classic after feeling something in his abdomen/oblique. Phillies manager Rob Thomson said it’s “very mild.”
Broke the second metatarsal in his right foot in a March 9 game in Ft. Myers. Expected to have surgery in the coming days.

144.status: Murphy took batting practice on the field on March 9. Atlanta is hoping the 2023 All-Star catcher returns from hip surgery during the first half of May.
Wentz will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Waldrep underwent arthroscopic surgery on Feb. 23 to have loose bodies removed from his right elbow. He could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on his return.
Schwellenbach underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. There is a chance he could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on his return.
Jiménez missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.
Underwent surgery on his right elbow on June 9.

145.status: Left his March 14 start with a trainer after throwing a pitch with two outs in the fourth inning. Will undergo further examination.
Suffered a Grade 2 strain playing with Team Italy on March 10 in World Baseball Classic. Officially returned to White Sox on March 12 to continue treatment. Will open the season on IL.

Hasn't played in a Cactus League game since March 6, when he sustained the injury throwing to bases. Had imaging done on March 10, with something showing up in there, according to manager Will Venable. Will be checked out by specialists for further treatment.
Threw a pair of 20-pitch, all-fastball bullpens, with the second coming March 6. Felt great after both. Tendinitis kept him off the mound through the first three weeks of camp.
Exited with a 3-2 count on Pablo Reyes during the seventh inning of a Cactus League game with the Padres on March 3.
Placed on 60-day IL as he continues to recover from Tommy John surgery he underwent last year. Has thrown numerous times off the mound.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team. Received non-roster invite to Spring Training.
Has been off the mound a few times and is feeling good in his recovery from Tommy John surgery he underwent March 7, 2025.

146.status: Imaging revealed severity after being scratched from March 11 lineup. Stretched his side making a backhanded play during individual pregame work that day.

Will begin to ramp up throwing intensity and throw to bases in coming days. Will continue to DH in Grapefruit League games with the expectation of playing defense during the final weekend of Spring Training.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.
Reassigned to Minor League camp on March 3. Felt discomfort during his Grapefruit League debut on Feb. 26.
Underwent core muscle surgery on Feb. 27. Sustained left groin injury during offseason workouts. Rested before arriving in Jupiter, where it flared up again.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Placed on 60-day IL on Feb. 12. Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.injury: Loose bodies, bone spur in left elbow
147.status: Tossed 30 pitches in live batting practice on March 14 after a 20-pitch session on March 9 -- his first time facing live hitters since October elbow surgery. Rodón could appear in at least one Grapefruit League game.

Is hitting overhand tosses and could advance to live at-bats on or around April 1. Volpe is participating in all defensive and baserunning drills and began diving on his left shoulder in early March. Underwent arthroscopic surgery Oct. 12.

Was facing hitters and throwing breaking balls as of March 6, when he threw two innings of live batting practice. Fastball has touched 97.5 mph. Could participate in Grapefruit League games this spring but will not be ready for Opening Day.
Tossing at distances of up to 90 feet on flat ground as of Feb. 27. Tentatively scheduled to begin throwing bullpens in late March. Could face hitters in April or May.
Recovering from February 2025 procedure. Has thrown eight bullpens as of mid-February. Said Feb. 14 he is not sure when game action will begin. "I'm taking it slow right now."

158.expectedReturn: No sooner than late April/early May
158.status: Diagnosed with an issue "in that T.O.S. [thoracic outlet syndrome] family" after visiting with a vascular specialist, according to Pat Murphy. The Brewers are hopeful it can be treated without surgery.
Threw a successful bullpen session on March 12 and could pitch in a game as soon as March 15.
Injured running to first base on March 9, and an MRI revealed a more serious injury than initially believed.
Being treated for a subscapularis strain.
A non-roster invitee to big league camp, Garabito had a bone fragment removed and a tendon repaired in his foot. He's expected to miss about four months.
A former top prospect who was in camp as a non-roster invitee, Garcia was injured playing winter ball.
